subject: Re: reconstructing non-CTF corrected tomograms
body:
Hi Karin,

thanks for the replay! :) It's actually for denoising with IsoNet2, but I 
would like to give cryoDRGN a go at some point as well- will let you know 
how it goes when I try it. 

Best,

Tamara

On Monday, February 2, 2026 at 2:32:06 PM UTC karin.m...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi )))
>
> I assume you’re looking into exporting your data to cryoDRGN? If yes 
> please let me know how it goes )) to answer your question: yes, you can. I 
> did it accidentally, while missing the ts_ctf step.
>
> Karine
>
> On Mon, Feb 2, 2026 at 21:22 Tamara Sijacki <tams...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi everyone!
>>
>>
>> I’d like to reconstruct my tomograms with WarpTools, but do the CTF 
>> correction using a different, external software.
>>
>> What’s the best way to do this? Can I just skip ts_ctf and run 
>> ts_reconstruct after tilt alignment or is there a specific flag I can use 
>> in e.g. ts_reconstruct?
>>
>>
>> Thanks in advance!
>>
>>
>> Best,
>>
>>
>> Tamara
>>
>> -- 
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
>> "Warp" group.

>> .
>>
>
